description: Numerical stability edge cases — extreme parameters/values for float32 vs float64 comparison
tolerance_note: Wider tolerance for extreme values where float32 (GenMLX/MLX) precision diverges from float64 (Gen.jl/Julia). For new distribution tests, tolerance may need to be 0.1 for lgamma-heavy computations at extreme arguments.

tests.note: Very peaked — large positive logpdf
Very flat — large negative logpdf
Far tail
Near boundary
Near boundary
Near zero
Large value
Large rate and value
Extreme logits
Large value, small rate
Extreme sigma — may overflow to Inf
Beta(1,1) = Uniform — logpdf should be 0.0
Large r: lgamma(150)-lgamma(51)-lgamma(100) requires cancellation of large lgamma values. Float32 may lose precision.
Small p: k*log(1-p) ~ k*(-p) for small p. log(0.99)~-0.01, so result ~ 10*(-0.01)+log(0.01) ~ -4.71.
Large k: lgamma(205) is very large. Tests lgamma precision for large arguments.
Very tight truncation. Phi(0.001)-Phi(-0.001) ~ 2*phi(0)*0.001 ~ 0.000798. log of small CDF difference is numerically sensitive.
Left truncation in far tail. Phi(3)-base is ~0.0013. Evaluating at the boundary tests log(Phi(100)-Phi(3)) ~ log(0.00135).
Wide truncation — should match Normal(0,1) exactly. Phi(100)-Phi(-100)~1.0. log-norm~0.
Large kappa: I0(100) is very large. log(I0) ~ kappa-0.5*log(2*pi*kappa). Tests bessel_i0e stability.
Small kappa: I0(kappa)~1, distribution approaches uniform on circle. log p ~ kappa*cos(x) - log(2*pi).
Large kappa at opposite point (x=pi, mu=0): cos(pi)=-1. log p = -50-log(2*pi)-log(I0(50)). Very negative.
Large df: lgamma values and (df*k/2)*log(2) are large. Tests cancellation precision.
Near-singular X: det(X)=1-0.9801=0.0199. log|X|~-3.917. Cholesky may be numerically sensitive.
df=k+2=4 is just above minimum df=k+1=3 for existence. lgamma((4-2+1)/2)=lgamma(1.5) near pole.
Large df, small X: tr(Psi*X^{-1})=10+10=20 (large). -(df+k+1)/2*log|X| also large.
Large range: log p = -log(1000001) ~ -13.816. Tests integer handling for large ranges.
Very small v: log(v)~-23.03 is far in left tail. z=-23.03, z^2~530. logpdf ~ 23.03-0.919-265 ~ -243.
Very small v: scale/v = 1e6 dominates. logpdf ~ -1e6. Tests for overflow to -Inf vs finite.
